title: Self-Learning Pipeline Integration

story.i_want_to: have the self-learning pipeline automatically trigger on every code review with visibility into its status
story.so_that: the knowledge wiki compounds automatically while I can monitor and control the pipeline

rationale: The ReviewKnowledgeWorker (US-21.1) and publish workflow (US-21.3) are building blocks, but they need to be wired into the existing review flow to form a complete self-learning pipeline. This story connects the worker to Progress.record_review/4 via Ecto.Multi (following the EventGenerator pattern), adds a tenant-level opt-in/opt-out setting, and provides a pipeline status endpoint so administrators can monitor extraction health. Together these pieces transform the review loop into a knowledge-compounding system with full observability.

acceptance_criteria.description: Progress.record_review/4 (or the equivalent review recording function) enqueues ReviewKnowledgeWorker in the same Ecto.Multi that creates the review_record. This follows the EventGenerator pattern already used for webhook events.

A tenant-level setting `settings.knowledge_auto_extract` (boolean, default true) controls whether the worker is enqueued. If false, the Multi step that enqueues the worker is skipped. The setting is read from the tenant record at review time.
The tenant settings field (jsonb) on the tenants table accommodates `knowledge_auto_extract`. If the settings field does not exist, a migration adds it as a jsonb column with default `{}`.
GET /api/v1/knowledge/pipeline endpoint exists (role: user+). Returns a JSON object with pipeline status metrics.
Pipeline status includes `pending_extractions`: count of ReviewKnowledgeWorker jobs in 'available' or 'scheduled' state for the tenant (queried from Oban jobs table).
Pipeline status includes `recent_drafts`: the 20 most recent draft articles with source_type: "review_finding", ordered by inserted_at desc. Each entry includes id, title, source_id, inserted_at.

Pipeline status includes `publish_rate`: ratio of published articles to total articles (published + draft) with source_type: "review_finding". Expressed as a float between 0.0 and 1.0. Returns 0.0 if no articles exist.
Pipeline status includes `extraction_errors`: count and most recent 5 failed/discarded ReviewKnowledgeWorker jobs for the tenant, including error reason and attempted_at.
Pipeline status includes `auto_extract_enabled`: the current value of the tenant's knowledge_auto_extract setting.
Idempotent: re-processing the same review_record does not create duplicate articles (enforced by the duplicate detection in US-21.1). Re-enqueuing the same job (e.g., via retry) is safe.
PUT /api/v1/tenant/settings endpoint (or PATCH to existing tenant endpoint) allows updating knowledge_auto_extract. Validates boolean type. Returns updated settings.
Pipeline endpoint enforces tenant scoping via RLS. Metrics reflect only the requesting tenant's data.

test_cases.name: Review recording enqueues knowledge worker when auto_extract enabled
steps: Check Oban jobs for the :knowledge queue
expected_results: ReviewKnowledgeWorker job enqueued with correct review_record_id
Review record is created in the same transaction

test_cases.name: Review recording skips worker when auto_extract disabled
steps: Check Oban jobs for the :knowledge queue
expected_results: Review record is still created successfully

test_cases.name: Pipeline status returns correct metrics
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant with auto_extract enabled

test_cases.name: Pipeline status shows extraction errors
preconditions: Discarded Oban jobs for ReviewKnowledgeWorker in the tenant
expected_results: extraction_errors includes count and recent failed jobs
Each error entry includes error reason and attempted_at

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: pipeline metrics only reflect own data
Update auto_extract setting via tenant settings endpoint
expected_results: Settings update returns 200 with updated settings

test_cases.name: Default auto_extract is true for new tenants
preconditions: A newly created tenant with no explicit settings
expected_results: Record review enqueues the knowledge worker

technical_notes: Follow the EventGenerator pattern exactly: add `Oban.insert(changeset)` as a named Multi step alongside the review_record insert. The worker job changeset uses `ReviewKnowledgeWorker.new(%{review_record_id: id, tenant_id: tenant_id})`.

Tenant settings: if a `settings` jsonb column already exists on tenants, simply add `knowledge_auto_extract` to it. If not, add a migration for the column. Access via `tenant.settings["knowledge_auto_extract"]` with a default of true when the key is missing.
Pipeline status queries Oban's `oban_jobs` table directly for job counts. Filter by worker name and tenant_id in the args. Use Oban.Job schema for queries.
Publish rate calculation: `published_count / (published_count + draft_count)`. Guard against division by zero by returning 0.0 when total is 0.
The pipeline endpoint is read-only and performs multiple queries. Consider combining them efficiently or accepting the N-query cost since this is a dashboard endpoint, not a hot path.
Testing: async: true, ConnCase for controller tests, DataCase for context tests, Mox for ExtractorBehaviour, fixture(:tenant) with settings, tenant isolation test, setup :verify_on_exit!.
